_schema: AutomationOS canonical agent safety policy. PARSER NOTE: the live agent gate (aibroker classify) uses a dumb scanner that reads every double-quoted token and bins it under the most-recently-seen section key among the three real arrays at the bottom: 'allow', 'require_approval', 'deny'. Therefore the EXACT tokens allow/require_approval/deny must appear NOWHERE except as those three array keys. All descriptive metadata above uses non-colliding key/value words (auto/auto_path/confirm/never) so it is binned to nobody (bin=-1) and ignored. classify precedence: DENY > REQUIRE_APPROVAL > ALLOW, deny-by-default; a token matches an exact tool name OR as a substring of the tool args.
_source_of_truth: Canonical auditable encoding of the policy the userspace agent gate currently hardcodes (aibroker policy_load_defaults; tool_write/codeagent path_write_allowed; agentd resolve_tool). aibroker LOADS this file in place of its built-in defaults. agentd does NOT read this file yet (its gate is compiled in via resolve_tool + bad_path); this file is the declared source of truth those built-ins should converge to.
_grant_full_doc: Session toggle. false = enforce the require_approval gate (confirm tools block for human-in-the-loop approval). true = flip every confirm tool to auto for the session. Operator-only; default MUST be false.

tools._doc: Vetted tool registry with default risk class. auto = run without prompting; auto_path = run only when the path passes path_rules; confirm = require human approval; never = always refused. Values intentionally avoid the words used as the array keys below.

_mouse_key_doc: mouse and key are synthetic-input GUI-takeover tools: always confirm because they seize the human pointer/keyboard.

path_rules._doc: Write/mutation path gate enforced by path_write_allowed (defense-in-depth, on top of classify). A path is writable only if it starts with a write_prefix, does not start with a protected prefix, and contains none of the reject_substrings. Protected wins over writable. Reject any path containing '..' (no traversal).
